rationale.P01: cert-gateway tls_handshake_failure_rate 0.97 vs 0.02, never auto-resolved, pat-tls-cert-expired surges from ~zero baseline to 4120 events (delta 9999%). The expired shared TLS cert is the single root of the handshake/5xx fanout. SEV1 root, must-page.
rationale.P02: payments 5xx surge sharing pat-tls-cert-expired, onset ~45s after P01. Downstream symptom of the one cert-expiry incident; collapse into P01, do not page separately.
rationale.P03: GC-pause CPU blip auto-resolved in 37s; metric back to 46 the next minute. pat-gc-pause neutral, delta 4%. Chronic churn.
rationale.P04: Disk flapper: 16 fires/hr oscillating around the 85% threshold, self-clears in 21s on log rotation; metric returns to 82. pat-disk-rotate delta 0%.
rationale.P05: api-edge handshake failures sharing pat-tls-cert-expired, onset ~80s after P01. Symptom of the cert-expiry incident; suppress as duplicate.
rationale.P06: frontend 5xx bump during fe-2026.05.20-c rollout, auto-resolved in 44s; metric back to 0.002. pat-rollout-503 neutral, delta 0%. Deploy transient.
rationale.P07: orders-db db_pool_wait_ms 8400 vs 250, rose 40->1900->8400->9600 with no recovery, pat-db-pool-exhausted surging 1800%. Independent SEV1 DB connection-pool exhaustion; different service and root pattern from the cert incident. Must-page.
rationale.P08: GC-pause CPU blip auto-resolved in 29s. Self-cleared, no sustained impact.
rationale.P09: checkout db_pool_wait_ms sharing pat-db-pool-exhausted, onset ~70s after P07. Downstream symptom of the DB pool-exhaustion incident; collapse into P07.
rationale.P10: Disk flapper (see P04), another oscillation: 16 fires/hr, cleared in 19s.
rationale.P11: webhook-dispatcher 5xx sharing pat-tls-cert-expired, same cert root and onset window. Symptom of the cert-expiry incident; suppress as duplicate.
rationale.P12: Cold-cache latency blip, self-resolved in 26s; metric back to 540. pat-cold-cache neutral.
rationale.P13: notifications queue marginally over threshold during batch drain, cleared in 17s; metric back to 3100. Routine.
rationale.P14: ingest-pipeline queue_depth 41000 vs 8000, monotonic rise 6200->41000->72000->98000 with no recovery, pat-ingest-backlog growing (delta 540%). Independent sustained backlog; different service and root from cert and DB incidents. SEV1, must-page.
rationale.P15: Cold-cache latency blip, recovered in 23s; metric back to 610.
rationale.P16: Post-deploy CPU settle during fe-2026.05.20-c, auto-resolved in 35s; metric back to 49. Deploy transient.
rationale.P17: media-encoder queue_depth matches already-open INC-9142 (pat-encoder-backlog) in incidents_open.json; oncall-media is already engaged and scaling out. Metric is flat-to-falling (9100->8600). Do not re-page owned work.
rationale.P18: Disk flapper (see P04), oscillation cleared in 20s.
rationale.P19: GC-pause CPU blip auto-resolved in 31s; metric back to 48.
rationale.P20: notifications 5xx flicker barely over threshold, self-cleared in 18s; metric back to 0.004.

notes: Three page-class incidents among 20 alerts. (1) Cert-expiry fanout: P01 cert-gateway is the SEV1 root; P02/P05/P11 share pat-tls-cert-expired within a ~2-minute onset window and are suppressed as duplicates of the one root (paging all four = over-paging). (2) DB connection-pool exhaustion: P07 orders-db SEV1 root; P09 checkout shares pat-db-pool-exhausted and is the downstream symptom, suppressed. (3) Sustained ingest backlog: P14, monotonic rise that never resolves. All three are must_page (miss_budget 0). Everything else is noise: disk flappers (P04/P10/P18, 16 fires/hr, ~20s auto-resolve), sub-minute GC/cold-cache/batch transients (P03/P08/P12/P13/P15/P19/P20), deploy-correlated rollout blips that recovered (P06/P16), and P17 which duplicates already-open INC-9142. MEDIUM: precision trap is the correlated cert/DB symptoms and the open-incident dup that look real (SEV2/SEV3, high values) but must not page; recall is protected because all real roots are must_page.
